operating_state: Total revenue increased 16% to $9.4 billion driven by Telxius acquisition adding 31,000 communications sites across Europe and Latin America and CoreSite acquisition establishing new Data Centers segment. Property operations generated 97% of total revenue with growth across all segments and broad international expansion. Three major tenants T-Mobile, AT&T, and Verizon represent 52% of consolidated revenues creating significant customer concentration. Churn remained elevated in Asia-Pacific due to carrier consolidation in India and expected multi-year elevated churn from T-Mobile master lease agreement cancellations.
cost_structure: Property gross margin expanded 13% with operational leverage allowing substantial majority of incremental revenue to flow through despite higher direct expenses from acquisitions. Depreciation, amortization and accretion increased 24% reflecting expanded asset base, while acquisition-related expenses surged due to major transactions. Ground lease costs remained primarily fixed under long-term agreements with annual escalations. Operating expenses are denominated 52% in foreign currencies creating translation exposure.
material_events: Completed Telxius acquisition adding European and Latin American tower assets and CoreSite acquisition establishing data center operations. Issued senior notes and raised equity through public offering to finance acquisitions. Completed ATC Europe transactions with CDPQ and Allianz acquiring 48% noncontrolling interests while retaining controlling ownership. Launched operations in Spain, Philippines, and Bangladesh through acquisitions and new construction.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments generated gains versus prior-year losses, with 44% of revenues and 52% of operating expenses denominated in foreign currencies. Indian operations face ongoing regulatory uncertainty from Supreme Court AGR rulings affecting tenant creditworthiness. Rising interest rates impact variable rate debt exposure and refinancing costs for near-term maturities.
forward_outlook: Management expects substantial non-cancellable tenant lease revenue from existing contracts providing revenue visibility, with anticipated consistent demand driven by 5G deployments and network densification. Projected capital expenditures include construction of communications sites globally and data center expansion targeting growing interconnected solutions demand. Must satisfy REIT distribution requirements of at least 90% of taxable income while maintaining debt service coverage ratios.
uncertainty_profile: Outstanding indebtedness requires refinancing of near-term maturities while maintaining covenant compliance with total leverage permitted up to 7.50x temporarily before stepping down. Integration risks from recent acquisitions including CoreSite data center operations and Telxius international expansion across multiple regulatory environments. Securitization structures require maintaining minimum debt service coverage ratios to avoid cash trapping affecting capital expenditure funding and REIT distribution capabilities.
